twitter_thread.hook: Unpopular opinion: most entrepreneurs should stop trying to Gumroad stores manually and just automate it 🧵
tweets: Here's the problem with manual Gumroad stores: it requires consistency. Humans aren't consistent. Systems are.
The 80/20 of automation: Set up the system once. Review monthly. Edit quarterly. That's it.
Most people quit at month 2. That's exactly when compounding starts. The bots don't quit.
Our system handles Gumroad stores for $2/day in API costs. A human equivalent costs $3k-5k/month.
Full breakdown at nyspotlightreport.com/blog/ — built this over 6 months. Happy to share specifics.

What would you automate first if you could?
